Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: UI: Navbar and footer icons lack hover states, inconsistent with theme
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
### Description
Interactive icons in both the navbar (Discord, GitHub, Twitter) and footer currently have no hover effects.

### Current behavior
- Hovering over navbar icons shows no visual change.
- Footer icons behave the same way.

### Expected behavior
- Icons should have hover states consistent with the site's dark + gold theme.
- Possible hover behaviors:
  - Color change or highlight
  - Subtle glow or shadow
  - Slight scale-up animation

### Why this matters
- Hover feedback is a core desktop UX expectation.
- Improves usability and accessibility.
- Maintains consistency between navbar and footer components.
- Enhances the premium look and feel of the site.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open https://djed.one
2. Hover over any icon in the navbar
3. Scroll to footer and hover over icons
4. Observe no hover feedback

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Refactor: Replace duplicate .replaceAll(',', '') calls with utility function
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
## Problem
The codebase has 18 duplicate instances of `.replaceAll(',', '')` scattered across multiple files to parse comma-formatted number strings. This repetition:
- Reduces code maintainability
- Makes future changes error-prone
- Violates DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle
- Increases bundle size unnecessarily

## Affected Files
- `src/utils/helpers.js` - 5 instances
- `src/routes/reservecoin.jsx` - 5 instances  
- `src/routes/stablecoin.jsx` - 1 instance
- `src/routes/protocol.jsx` - 3 instances
- `src/routes/my-balance.jsx` - 3 instances
- `src/components/molecules/CoinCard/CoinCard.jsx` - 1 instance (commented)

## Proposed Solution
Create a centralized `parseFormattedNumber()` utility function in `helpers.js` and replace all instances of `.replaceAll(',', '')` with this function.

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth for number parsing
- Easier to modify parsing logic in the future
- Better code readability
- Improved maintainability

## Implementation Details
1. Add `parseFormattedNumber(formattedString)` function to `src/utils/helpers.js`
2. Replace all `.replaceAll(',', '')` calls with the new utility
3. Add proper JSDoc documentation
4. Ensure no functional changes (pure refactoring)

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: 86/inconsistent message fix
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Fix: Inconsistent Reserve Coin Sell Message
Problem #86 
When users attempted to sell Reserve Coin, they received a generic error message "Reserve ratio would drop below the minimum" regardless of the actual scenario. This created confusion because the message didn't distinguish between:

The reserve ratio already being below the minimum requirement
The transaction would cause the reserve ratio to drop below the minimum
Solution
Enhanced the validation logic to provide distinct, context-aware error messages:

Current State Check: Added validation to check if the reserve ratio is already below the minimum before processing the transaction
Future State Check: Existing logic now only triggers if current state is acceptable, checking if the transaction would cause the ratio to drop
Changes Made
1. 
src/utils/constants.js
Added two new transaction validity constants:

RESERVE_RATIO_ALREADY_LOW: Displayed when the system is currently in a restricted state
RESERVE_RATIO_WOULD_DROP_LOW: Displayed when the transaction would cause the restriction
2. 
src/routes/reservecoin.jsx
Enhanced 
updateSellTradeData()
 function:

Added current reserve ratio validation using current blockchain state
Updated validation order to check current ratio before future ratio
Assigned appropriate error messages based on the specific scenario
User Impact
✅ Clear Communication: Users now understand exactly why their transaction is blocked
✅ Better UX: Distinct messages for different scenarios reduce confusion
✅ Transparency: Users can distinguish between systemic restrictions vs transaction-specific issues

Testing
 Code review completed
 Validation logic verified
 Manual testing in development environment recommended
Files Changed
src/utils/constants.js
 (+2 new constants)
src/routes/reservecoin.jsx
 (~15 lines modified in validation logic)

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: CSS Error
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
This update corrects common CSS/SCSS issues that were causing inconsistent styling and visibility problems across the application.

Changes

Standardized color usage by relying on CSS variables for runtime styling

Added fallback values to CSS variables to prevent invisible text and icons

Removed error-prone mixing of SCSS variables and CSS variables

Reduced global style side effects by limiting broad selectors

Ensured predictable link and icon coloring across light and dark backgrounds

Result

No invisible icons or text

More reliable runtime theming

Safer, more maintainable CSS without changing visual design

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Improve SEO for Djed keyword ranking
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
# Improve SEO for Djed Keyword Ranking

## 🎯 Objective

Improve the search engine ranking of Milkomeda Djed (djed.one) so that it appears before djed.xyz when users search for the keyword "djed" on Google. Currently, djed.xyz shows up as the top result, while djed.one does not.

## 📋 Changes Overview

This PR implements comprehensive SEO optimizations to increase visibility and improve Google Search ranking for branded queries related to Djed. All metadata, indexing, and content signals now align with SEO best practices.

## ✨ Key Improvements

### 1. **Enhanced Meta Tags**
- ✅ Fixed empty `<title>` tag with SEO-optimized title featuring "Djed" keyword prominently
- ✅ Added comprehensive meta description optimized for "djed" keyword searches
- ✅ Implemented keywords meta tag with relevant Djed-related terms
- ✅ Added proper robots and googlebot meta tags for optimal crawling

### 2. **Structured Data (Schema.org)**
- ✅ Added JSON-LD structured data for rich snippets
- ✅ Implemented WebApplication schema with organization information
- ✅ Included social media links and provider information

### 3. **Sitemap & Robots**
- ✅ Created `sitemap.xml` with all major pages for better search engine indexing
- ✅ Updated `robots.txt` with sitemap reference and proper crawl directives
- ✅ Configured canonical URLs for all pages to prevent duplicate content issues

### 4. **Open Graph & Social Media**

Our objective is to **improve the search engine ranking** of [https://djed.one/](https://djed.one/) so that it appears **before `djed.xyz`** when users search for the keyword **"djed"** on Google.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Issue: Hardcoded Values and Dead Code Smells in `checkBuyableRc`, `checkSellableRc`, and `getCoinBudgets`
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
The functions `checkBuyableRc` and `checkSellableRc` currently return hardcoded values that automatically approve transactions, which should not be the case. Additionally, the `getCoinBudgets` function is returning hardcoded `null` values. These hardcoded values need to be either removed or commented out to ensure the correct logic is implemented.

Furthermore, these hardcoded values introduce **dead code smells**, as they do not contribute meaningfully to the program's functionality. Keeping such code can increase complexity and reduce maintainability. Removing or refactoring these sections will improve code quality and readability.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Sepolia frontend changes to make sure stablecoin transactions are accurate displaying the prices
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Previously, users trying to obtain 10,000 stablecoins would see a value of **0 ETH**, which is inaccurate. The issue stemmed from the parameters configured for the conversion function, particularly with the decimal points (`scDecimals, rcDecimals, and BC_decimals`).

Sepolia showed a value of 0.000000000000000032 ETH(18 digits) due to incorrect BC_decimals being used (**18)**, which, when displayed on the frontend, only showed 0.00000 ETH.

- Introduced decimal_subtractor = 12 in sepolia.env to adjust the decimal precision correctly:
When BC_decimals is set to 18 in the conversion function but scDecimals = 6 is required, subtract decimal_subtractor from BC_decimals (18 - 12 = 6).

- For Milkomeda network, set decimal_subtractor = 0:
This way, 18 - 0 = 18 decimal points remain unchanged, ensuring that Milkomeda continues working as before.

Resolves #113

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Configure Sepolia Frontend for Transaction Price Display
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Objective:
Ensure Correct Fetching and Display of Transaction Prices: 
The Sepolia frontend must accurately fetch and display the current transaction prices for Stablecoin and Reservecoin transactions. This involves correctly interpreting and applying the` rcDecimals`, `scDecimals`, and `BC_decimals` parameters to calculate and present transaction costs accurately.


Current Behavior:
Incorrect Estimation of Transaction Costs: 
Users attempting to obtain 10,000 Stablecoins are currently being estimated to require 0 ETH for the transaction. This discrepancy indicates a fundamental issue with how the `rcDecimals`, `scDecimals`, and `BC_decimals` parameters are configured or utilized within the frontend's calculation logic.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Implement tab activity detection to optimize  API calls
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
### **Overview:**
This resolves #105 
Previously, the application was fetching the price at regular intervals, regardless of whether the user's tab was active or not. This approach led to reaching the daily limit of the free Infura API when deployed for Sepolia, as a single user could leave a tab open, consuming the limit.

Implemented a solution that detects whether the tab is active or not. The price update logic now only executes when the tab is active, significantly reducing the number of API calls and preventing the daily limit from being reached.

This change ensures that the application remains functional and efficient under the constraints of the free Infura API, while also improving the user experience by reducing unnecessary data fetching when the application is not in use.

###  Detailed Description:

| Before Changes | After Changes |
|:---:|:---:|
| ![before changes](https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/assets/157852043/22baf38c-a0d7-4477-9169-c0abd05a22b1) | ![after changes](https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/assets/157852043/317f029a-93c7-4f09-8f1b-5e5e224a1fe6) |

As shown in the images ,Previously, even when the tab was not active and the user had switched to another tab, there were **10 request methods** being called every minute. This behaviour could easily lead to exceeding the API rate limit.

implementing a solution that involves detecting whether the tab is active. As a result,  **no requests (zero)** are being made when the tab is not active.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
## Pull Request Title: Improved transaction verification in verifyTx function
## Fixes: #104 
 
## Issue:

The original verifyTx function only checked the transaction status once after a fixed delay, leading to unreliable verification.

## Fix:

Refactored the verifyTx function to continuously check the transaction status until confirmation or rejection. Implemented recursive checking using setTimeout for regular status updates.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Refreshing prices frequently causes Infura's free API limits to be reached
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body: We didn't have a problem when we had only a deployment for Milkomeda, because the Milkomeda API is free. But, with the deployment for Sepolia, the Infura API has a limit of calls in its free API. It suffices for a single user to forget a tab with https://sepolia.djed.one open for the daily limit to be almost reached. This occurs because we are fetching the price at regular intervals, even when the user's tab is inactive. Ideally, we should detect whether the tab is active or not and only update the price when it is active.

This commit addresses these issues and ensures accurate price display across all networks.Fixes #97

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Implement a StablePay Button Widget
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Many merchants would like to enable crypto payments, but this is currently hard to do. Existing solutions (e.g. "nowpayments") charge large fees from the merchants and customers,  rely on centralized backends and are operated by companies who serve as centralized gatekeepers deciding which tokens get listed as available for payments.

Most stablecoins live isolated in the world of DeFI and are not really adopted by merchants and customers. By making a "StablePay" widget that merchants could easily integrate in their websites, Djed stablecoins could become the preferred stablecoins for payments, boosting their adoption.

This widget should be similar in functionality and developer experience as a "Pay with PayPal" widget.

The widget should allow the customer to connect his wallet, choose an EVM-compatible network supported by Djed (currently only Milkomeda, but more are coming...), and then pay either in the native currency of that network or using a Djed stablecoin in that network. 

We should create a separate repo for this widget. But I'm describing this task in this repo for now, because much of the functionality could probably be borrowed from this repo. We will create a new repo, once we assign someone to this issue.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: removed wallet support other than metamask temporarily
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Current behaviour: when the user clicks on "connect wallet", several wallets appear.

Desired behaviour: when the user clicks on "connect wallet", only Metamask should appear.

The desired behaviour is achieved with the minimum amount of code changes possible because we would like to reactivate all the wallets in the future when the WSC integration work is complete.

Updated Readme file for easy setup locally
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Added the instructions for setting up the .env file

Setting the env file

    Go to the 'env' folder.
    Open the 'milkomeda-c1-testnet' file, select all the text, and copy it.
    Go back to your project's main folder.
    Create a new file in that folder.
    Paste the copied text into the new file.
    Name the new file '.env'.

```

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Inconsistent message when attempting to sell Reserve Coin despite the reserve ratio being below the minimum already.
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
![image](https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/assets/98951143/71f02f35-af9b-4e9e-b4dc-f2148be96a65)

Inconsistent message when attempting to sell Reserve Coin despite the reserve ratio being below the minimum already.

Currently, when a user attempts to sell Reserve Coin, they receive a generic message regardless of the reserve ratio being below the minimum requirement. This inconsistency creates confusion and could lead to misunderstanding of the actual issue.

Example: 
Reserve Ratio Below Minimum

Apologies, but selling Reserve Coin is currently restricted due to the reserve ratio being below the minimum requirement. Proceeding with the transaction would further decrease the reserve ratio.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Make the Web Dashboard Generic
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
The current Web Dashboard is specific for Milkomeda-C1. Titles, headings and paragraphs all have hard-coded content specific for Milkomeda-C1.

We would like to make it generic so that it can easily be used for deployments of Djed on other EVM-compatible blockchains.

This means that all content that is currently hard-coded should be moved into variables in a separate configuration or environment file. And the pages should just read the content from these variables.

Note that we already have different environment files "milkomeda-c1.env" and "milkomeda-c1-testnet.env". In the future, we will have other environment variables for other blockchains as well.  So, maybe the simplest solution for this issue would be to move the content into these environment files. But there might be more elegant solutions.

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Migrate to wagmi and integrate Flint Wallet
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
Here are some changed I made on this PR:

1. I needed to fix some peer dependencies issues when installing wagmi, since the project has create-react-app on v4 and there were some conflicts with typescript dependency. Plus, I needed to unified browserlist development and production because of this [issue](https://github.com/facebook/create-react-app/issues/9468#issuecomment-694191642)

3. I did some refactor to start using wagmi to manage multiple wallet connections in an easier way. This library provides a bunch of handy react hooks, functions to connect wallet, balance, interact with smart contract, sign transactions and some other performance/handling errors improvements. You can find more on their docs https://wagmi.sh/

4. I had to do some UI tweaks for desktop version in order to display metamask and flint but please feel free to adapt the UI and also improve mobile version.

Let me know if you have any questions or suggestions. Here's a short demo about the integration

Demo: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Sl_MpQewzB9WRL_hUEkJJbBEVIi25HWX/view?usp=sharing

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: FE: Calculate future scPrice & update ratio calculations
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
### 1.Write function that calculates future sc price*

### Formula from DJED contract:


![Image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42462482/221826877-af1a2e86-2bf6-4964-9f15-4dfa0b89bbd9.png)

   
   ### FE calculates the following parameters in different way:

   **sSC = stableCoin.totalSupply() + amountSC;**
   **R(_currentPaymentAmount) = R(0) + amountBC**
   ***amountSC** - if transaction is buy stableCoin, this is the amount of stablecoin that user wants to buy. If transaction is for reservecoins, this param should be 0;
   ***amountBC** - amount of stable/reserve coin converted to base coin (mADA) without calculating fees.


### 2.Update ratio calculations to use future sc price for buy/sell reservecoin and buy stablecoin,** 
Ratio functions stay the same, we're just using different value for the scPrice in our formula. Now we should use futureScPrice calculated with above formula.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42462482/221823759-bc40e6ef-8fb6-40e9-9a9c-cc0fbec46bcc.png)

### 3.The frontend should use the Djed address for interaction with Oracle contract, in order to be authorized to read data from it. 

### Update sc & rc links on home page

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: FE: Hide current ratio when totalSupply is zero
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body: ![Image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42462482/208891831-9bfc382d-469e-48df-bf96-8e8fd058ce31.png)

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: FE: Sell RC - Cannot fetch RC price when totalSupply is zero
FE validation
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
FE validation: 
 [Issue description can also be found here](https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/issues/9)

Test cases

- [x] -Do not allow transactions above the user BC balance (buy SC/RC)
- [x] -Do not allow transactions above the user SC/RC balance (sell SC/RC)
- [x] -Do not allow transactions below the min transaction limit.

MIN TX VALIDATION:

- Buy SC

```
function isRatioAboveMin(uint256 _scPrice) internal view returns (bool) {
        return
            R(0) * scalingFactor * scDecimalScalingFactor >
            stableCoin.totalSupply() * _scPrice * reserveRatioMin;
   }
```
Where stableCoin.totalSupply() = stableCoin.totalSupply() + amountSC
Where  R(0) = R() + amountBC +  amountBC * fee / scalingFactor 
Where amountBC = amountSC * scPrice / scDecimalScalingFactor
*amountSC - Amount of SC that user wants to buy
*amountBC - amountSC converted to base coin
*fee is currently 1%

- Sell SC - No validation for min tx limit

- Buy RC
```
function isRatioBelowMax(uint256 _scPrice) internal view returns (bool) {
        return
            R(0) * scalingFactor * scDecimalScalingFactor <
            stableCoin.totalSupply() * _scPrice * reserveRatioMax  || stableCoin.totalSupply() <= thresholdSupplySC
    }
```
Where  R(0) = R() + amountBC +  amountBC * fee / scalingFactor 
Where amountBC = amountRC * rcBuyingPrice / rcDecimalScalingFactor
*amountRC - Amount of RC that user wants to buy
*amountBC - amountRC converted to base coin
*fee is currently 1%

- Sell RC
Same formula as for buy SC
```
function isRatioAboveMin(uint256 _scPrice) internal view returns (bool) {
        return
            R(0) * scalingFactor * scDecimalScalingFactor >
            stableCoin.totalSupply() * _scPrice * reserveRatioMin;
   }
```

Where  R(0) = R() - ( amountBC -  amountBC * fee / scalingFactor)
Where amountBC = amountRC * rcTargetPrice / rcDecimalScalingFactor
*amountRC - Amount of RC that user wants to buy
*amountBC - amountRC converted to base coin
*fee is currently 1%


**How to test**
Use the following functions to calculate amount that will cause the ratio limit error


![Image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42462482/209804227-9e163891-c5f7-47df-b7d4-9c7cbc33695a.png)

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: QA - Functional Test Cases
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
[Milkomeda Djed - Manual Test Report](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1osyFiZECnsZ-tEavVw_08shvIZo6xpH8J2XZQ0e5paI/edit#)
[Milkomeda Djed - Test Cases](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1KlO0PVgYujiDupq11XD2M_LQe6zfiIIY9pcfPYvkDPQ/edit?pli=1#gid=0)

Document is covering **Milkomeda Djed App** test cases for all current pages and functionalities on the App itself, i.e.:
- `Protocol` Page
- `StableDjed` Page
- `ReservedDjed` Page
- `My Balance` Page

Different user types mentioned within the document:
- `Guest` -> user not connected to his **Metamask** wallet
- `User With Wallet` -> user connected to his **Metamask** wallet
- `All Users` -> expected behavior for any user

Transaction Limits Calculation:
- Formula used for calculating value of payment with fees: amountBC / (1 - totalFees / 100)
- `amountBC = Current Price value * amount entered in the field`
- `totalFees` = 1.44%
- 1 milktADA token = 10$

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Fleek setup
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
This PR will resolve issue #12 

Changes:
* Moved `env` folder to root directory
* Added build scripts for each chain (env)
* Using `env-cmd` package in the build script to load environment variables depending on which chain we are deploying
* Deployed testnet and mainnet using these build scripts via fleek, on the `fleek-setup` branch.

PS - Will update the branch on Fleek, once we merge this in the main.

![](https://i.imgur.com/voIuUT1.png)

In the future, we will have multiple production deployments and the "local.env" file is curently using non-local testnet values for the variables. Therefore, I would recommend renaming:

* "local.env" to "milkomeda-c1-testnet.env"
* "production.env" to "milkomeda-c1.env"

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: FE: Transaction validation
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
**Request**
Allow user to buy/sell stablecoin and reservecoin up to amount less then 10000$. 

According to the Djed contract functions and transaction limit validation, FE will compare the following values depending on transaction type and coin:

- **Buy/Sell stablecoins**
It should be simply the number of stablecoins that the user wants to buy/sell and that he/she inputs in the text field.  For example, if the user writes that he/she wants to buy/sell 5000 stablecoins, the frontend should check that 5000 < 10000.

- **Buy reservecoin**
It should be the number of reservecoins that the user wants to buy and that he/she inputs in the text field + fees.
For example: User wants to buy 1 reservecoin, we convert it to BC and append fees which is for example 1.155 BC. That amount in USD should be < 10000USD

- **Sell reservecoin**
It should be the number of reservecoins that the user wants to sell and that he/she inputs in the text field without fees.
For example: User wants to sell 1 reservecoin, we convert it to BC which is for example 1.139 BC. That amount in USD should be < 1000USD


**UI changes**
If that limit is reached, the submit button should be disabled and user should see a message "Transaction limit exceeded"

Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.title: Make the frontend compatible with the new version of the Djed contracts
Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard.body:
The Djed contracts have changed significantly, and the frontend is not compatible anymore. The goal of this task is to make the frontend compatible again.

### Latest Mutually Compatible Versions:

The last commits where Djed, the oracle and the frontend were compatible with each other are:

* Frontend: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/commit/b0915f5371db4148a916294a7325ebcdd1ad79a7 (this is the head of the `development` branch: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/tree/development)

* Oracle: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Oracle-Solidity/commit/4c4263d6527621fbc1e80064fcc6c2fa92106d5f

* Djed: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity/commit/96d1012a02c089bef87c61eabb89a0df8cc10a07

Instructions to deploy and run all these three components together at that point in time can be found at: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity/tree/96d1012a02c089bef87c61eabb89a0df8cc10a07/docs .

### Summary of Djed's Changes (and What To Do About Them)

* The interface of the public functions for buying and selling stablecoins (SCs) and reservecoins (RCs) changed. Each of these functions now has 3 new arguments.
    *   for the `receiver` argument, the frontend should simply pass `msg.sender` (i.e. the address of the user whose wallet is connected to the frontend) as the value.
    *   for the `ui` argument, the frontend should read an address from a configuration file. For testing purposes, just include an arbitrary address in the configuration file for now.
    *   for the `fee_ui` argument, the frontend should read a value from a configuration file. For testing purposes, include a value representing 0.2% in the configuration file for now.  (Pay attention to the scaling factor used by the Djed contracts to represent decimal numbers by `uint`s.)

* Several auxiliary functions were removed from the Djed contract. These functions were used by the frontend to calculate the maximum number of coins that could be bought or sold and to compute the prices of buying or selling N of these coins.
   *  The maximum check functionality should be simply removed. If the user tries to buy more than the maximum, the transaction will simply revert.  (Note: the removal of this functionality was already done. See next section.)
   *  To compute the price of buying or selling N coins, the frontend should read the price of 1 coin from the contract and multiply the value read by N. This should be done at the frontend side. 

* The names of the contract functions that return the price of stablecoins and reservecoins changed and their interfaces may have changed too.
   *  The frontend should use the new functions.

* The documentation was moved away from https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity/tree/96d1012a02c089bef87c61eabb89a0df8cc10a07/docs . Documentation that was related to the oracle can now be found in the README of the oracle repo. Documentation that was related to Djed can now be found in the README of the Djed repo.

* The oracle poster scripts were moved away from the Djed repo and into the Oracle repo.

### Summary of Changes Already Made to the Frontend

* The removal of the dependency of the frontend on the auxiliary functions of the old contract was already done here (https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/tree/auxiliary-fxns-removal), but it hasn't been tested it.
* The removal of the maximum check functionality was done in this branch (https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/tree/max-button-removal), but it hasn't been tested yet.
* Additional work towards making the frontend compatible with the new version was done here (https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/tree/new-interfaces). Everything in this branch is very much WIP.


### Branch

Work on this task should be done in the `new-interfaces` branch (https://github.com/DjedAlliance/Djed-Solidity-WebDashboard/tree/new-interfaces).


### Potential Challenges

The oracle contract changed too. The new Djed contracts depend on the new oracle. The new oracle has not been tested yet. The poster scripts still have not been adapted to work with the new oracle contracts. Fortunately, the frontend doesn't depend on the new oracle directly. Therefore, I don't expect these oracle issues to be an obstacle for this task. Nevertheless, unexpected challenges related to the oracle could arise. If this occurs, consider deploying Djed using the `MockOracle` available in the Djed repo.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: [BUG]: Error 403 on fetching deeper blocks of sepolia blockchain
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body: ### Bug Description We are using a free public RPC node for Sepolia (ethereum-sepolia.publicnode.com). When the app tries to fetch historical transactions deep in the past, it asks for "archive data". Free nodes usually block these deep historical queries and demand an API key (as stated in the error: "Archive requests require a personal token"). ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Run with `npm run dev` 2. Click fetch transactions button 3. Go to the console and you will see the error ### Logs and Screenshots ### Environment Details - OS: Mac - Browser: Chrome ### Impact Medium - Feature works but has issues ### Code of Conduct - [x] I have joined the [Discord server](https://discord.gg/hjUhu33uAn) and will post updates there - [x] I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: fix: Added persistent caching
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
###  Addressed Issues:
<!-- Link the issue this PR addresses -->
Fixes #6 


###  Screenshots/Recordings:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30fcfccb-3de4-4132-bef9-e95538634ad8




###  Additional Notes:

Description - 
This PR significantly optimizes how the merchant dashboard handles blockchain data by introducing persistent caching and incremental fetching.

Changes made - 
1. Persistent Caching (Indefinite)
- Cached fetched transactions in localStorage securely so they persist across page reloads and user sessions.
- Removed the 5-minute expiry rule—the cache is now indefinite until explicitly cleared by the user.
- Cached state tracks the lastSyncedBlocks on a per-network basis (Sepolia, ETC Mainnet, Mordor).
2. Incremental Fetching Mechanism
- The dashboard now leverages fromBlockOverride to resume fetching exactly where it left off, rather than scanning from block 0 (or DEPLOYMENT_BLOCKS).
- Appends new transactions to the existing cache and deduplicates them using transactionHash.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: feat: Add pagination and sorting to Transactions page
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
###  Addressed Issues:
Fixes #31 


###  Screenshots/Recordings:

UI pagination and sorting

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/919be258-4d9c-4a59-8ba6-b7550a4d5aea


Fetch pagination


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f315f1a-49bd-4b5d-9a5d-ce1293ec6790





###  Additional Notes:
Changes made
- Added pagination, page sizes available are 25, 50, 100, 200.
- Added sort feature, you can now sort by timestamp, blockchain number, amountSC, and amountBC in ascending or descending order.
- Added pagination fetching, transactions show up as they are fetched.

Performance optimization in fetching timestamps
- Since fetching timestamps requires an additional API call for the block, the timestamps will only be fetched when sorted by timestamp.
- Timestamps of transactions of the same block are fetched at once.

IMPORTANT 
- Capped fetching at 1k blocks, if the user wants to fetch more they must click the fetch more button.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: fix: Separate merchant address settings for dev and prod
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
###  Description

Issue: 
- In a development environment, a developer may not have the required number of transactions to test a feature or fix a bug. 
- Such a developer has to change merchant_address to '' every time he/she wants to develop a new feature.

Changes:
- `npm run dev` will now use the dev environment, which will automatically set merchant_address to '', i.e., it will fetch all transactions
- Production build will still work by connecting the wallet and fetching only the transactions of the connected wallet.

How to test
- Run `npm run dev` for the development environment and see all transactions being fetched
- Run `npm run build && npx serve out` for the production environment and see only your transactions

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: [FEATURE]: Add Open Graph metadata and Twitter Card tags for better social sharing
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
### Feature and its Use Cases

Currently the StablePay dashboard lacks Open Graph and Twitter Card metadata, 
so when the app link is shared on platforms like Discord, Twitter, or Telegram, 
no rich preview card is shown — just a plain URL.

Adding proper OG and Twitter Card meta tags would make the app look more 
professional and discoverable when shared on social media.

### Additional Context

This PR is in line with the AOSSIE GSoC 2026 SEO and Marketing idea which 
aims to improve discoverability across AOSSIE projects by adding Open Graph 
metadata, social sharing features, and SEO best practices. This is a first 
step — adding metadata to StablePay as a starting point for the broader initiative.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Bug: Function Components Cannot Be Given Refs in Button Component
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body: Description The Button component in the project throws a warning when used in certain contexts. The warning indicates that function components cannot be given refs, which occurs because the Button component is not wrapped in React.forwardRef. How to Reproduce 1. Go to transactions page 2. Click fetch transactions 3. Observe the error in the console. Actual behaviour Following error is displayed in console ``` Warning: Function components cannot be given refs. Attempts to access this ref will fail. Did you mean to use React.forwardRef()? Check the render method of `SlotClone`. Error Component Stack at Button (button.tsx:41:3) at DashboardPageLayout (index.tsx:14:3) at TransactionsPage (page.tsx:43:102) at TooltipProvider (tooltip.tsx:9:3) at SidebarProvider (sidebar.tsx:46:3) at Providers (providers.tsx:9:29) at body () at html () at RootLayout [Server] () ``` Screenshot Expected Behavior The Button component should handle refs properly without throwing any warnings. It should be wrapped in React.forwardRef to allow refs to be passed down correctly. Potential Fix Wrap the Button component in React.forwardRef to handle refs. Here’s an example of the fix: ``` const Button = React.forwardRef< HTMLButtonElement, React.ComponentProps<"button"> & VariantProps & { asChild?: boolean; } >(({ className, variant, size, asChild = false, ...props }, ref) => { const Comp = asChild ? Slot : "button"; return ( ); }); Button.displayName = "Button"; ``` This fix ensures that the Button component can accept refs and resolves the warning.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: feat: Implement Pagination and Infinite Scroll for Transactions
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
Fixes #31 

### Description
This PR addresses a critical scalability issue where the `TransactionService` was fetching the entire history of events from the blockchain on every load. As transaction volume grows, this behavior would lead to performance degradation and eventual application crashes. 

This update implements an efficient pagination system that fetches transactions in batches (scanning the blockchain backwards) and adds infinite scrolling to the UI.


### Changes made
1. In `dashboard/lib/transaction-service.ts` 
- Replaced the monolithic fetchStableCoinPurchases with a cursor-based `fetchTransactions` method.
- Implemented `fetchBatchEvents` to scan blockchain logs backwards in chunks (default 10k blocks), ensuring the most recent transactions loads immediately.
- Added logic to fetch block timestamps to correctly merge and sort events across multiple networks (Sepolia, ETC, Mordor).
- Introduced `FetchState`  to manage block cursors and event buffers for subsequent page loads.

2. In `dashboard/hooks/use-transactions.ts` 
- Updated the hook to manage the new pagination state cursors and buffers.
- Added loadMore  functionality for infinite scrolling.
- Enhanced local storage caching to persist pagination state, serialized BigInt block numbers, and transaction timestamps, allowing users to resume scrolling without re-fetching.

3. In `dashboard/app/transactions/page.tsx`
- Implemented an IntersectionObserver  sentinel at the bottom of the transaction table to trigger loadMore automatically when scrolling.
- Updated the UI to handle "loading more" states without blocking the existing view.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: feat(transactions): add wallet filtering and persistent incremental caching
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
### Problem
- Dashboard fetched ALL transactions from block 6M on every refresh.
- Cache expired every 5 minutes, causing wasteful RPC calls.
- Transactions weren't filtered by the connected merchant wallet.

### Solution
- **Wallet Filtering**: Only displays transactions where `receiver === connectedWallet`.
- **Persistent Caching**: removed expiry; cache persists indefinitely until manually cleared.
- **Incremental Sync**: "Fetch Fresh Data" only queries blocks from `lastSyncedBlock` to `currentBlock`.
- **Performance**: Reduces RPC calls by >90% for returning users.

### How to Test
1. Connect wallet on Dashboard.
2. Click "See Transactions" (first fetch).
3. Refresh page -> Verify transactions load instantly from cache.
4. Make a new transaction on Sepolia.
5. Click "Fetch Fresh Data" -> Verify only the new transaction is fetched.
6. Switch wallet -> Verify cache clears/switches context.

  * Wallet-aware transaction fetching with incremental and full refresh options, plus per-wallet cache and Clear Cache control.
  * Last sync time and block info shown in the header.

* **Improvements**
  * Robust handling of loading, error, empty and normal table states with consistent empty-row layout.
  * Updated transaction detail modal with clearer status, risk and field presentation.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: feat: wallet-based transaction filtering with persistent caching
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
# PR Title
```
feat: Wallet-based transaction filtering with persistent caching
```

# PR Description

## 🎯 Fixes #6

## Summary

Implements wallet-based transaction filtering and persistent caching to improve dashboard performance and reduce unnecessary blockchain queries.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           DATA FLOW DIAGRAM                                │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│  ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │   WALLET    │────────▶│  Merchant Addr   │────────▶│  localStorage  │  │
│  │   CONNECT   │         │    Captured      │         │   Persisted    │  │
│  └─────────────┘         └──────────────────┘         └────────────────┘  │
│                                                                            │
│  ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │   FETCH     │────────▶│  Filter by       │────────▶│    CACHE       │  │
│  │   DATA      │         │  receiver addr   │         │ + lastBlock    │  │
│  └─────────────┘         └──────────────────┘         └────────────────┘  │
│                                                                            │
│  ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │ FETCH FRESH │────────▶│  Start from      │────────▶│   APPEND +     │  │
│  │    DATA     │         │  lastSyncedBlock │         │   DEDUPE       │  │
│  └─────────────┘         └──────────────────┘         └────────────────┘  │
│                                                                            │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## ✨ Key Features

### 1. Transaction Filtering
- Filters transactions where connected wallet is the **receiver** (merchant)
- Merchant address captured on wallet connect
- Passed to `TransactionService` for blockchain-level filtering

### 2. Persistent Caching
- Transactions stored in localStorage with **indefinite retention**
- Cache keyed by merchant address (auto-invalidates on wallet change)
- Survives page refreshes and browser restarts

### 3. Incremental Fetching
- Tracks `lastSyncedBlock` for each merchant
- "Fetch Fresh Data" only queries blocks after last sync
- Deduplicates by transaction hash before appending
- Significantly reduces blockchain RPC calls

### 4. SSR Safety
- `getStoredMerchantAddress()` checks for browser environment
- No localStorage access during server-side rendering

---

## 📁 Files Changed

| File | Changes | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `hooks/use-wallet.ts` | +35 | Merchant address persistence |
| `hooks/use-transactions.ts` | +95 | Persistent cache + incremental fetch |
| `lib/transaction-service.ts` | +15 | Support `startFromBlock` parameter |
| `app/transactions/page.tsx` | +180 | UI updates + "Fetch Fresh Data" |
| `app/page.tsx` | +20 | Wallet connection guidance |

**Total: 5 files, ~350 lines of focused changes**

---

## 🖥️ UI Improvements

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  TRANSACTIONS PAGE                                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Transaction Network                                            │
│  Filtering by: 0x2881...e82F          [Fetch Fresh Data] [Clear]│
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ ⚠️ No transactions found for your wallet                │   │
│  │                                                          │   │
│  │ If you don't have any StablePay transactions:           │   │
│  │ • Make a transaction on the merchant demo website       │   │
│  │ • Visit mordor.djed.one to buy stablecoins              │   │
│  │ • Click "Fetch Fresh Data" after making a transaction   │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  Last synced: Block #7234567                                    │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Only merchant-relevant transactions are displayed
- [x] Cached transactions persist across refreshes
- [x] Incremental fetch works based on `lastSyncedBlock`
- [x] No previously fetched data is lost on refresh or refetch
- [x] Wallet connection prompts shown when not connected
- [x] Helpful guidance when no transactions found

---

## 🧪 Testing Checklist

- [x] Connect wallet → merchant address persisted to localStorage
- [x] Fetch transactions → filtered by receiver address
- [x] Page refresh → cached data loads instantly
- [x] "Fetch Fresh Data" → only new transactions fetched
- [x] Disconnect wallet → cache cleared
- [x] Different wallet → old cache invalidated
- [x] Build passes without errors

---

## 🔄 Cache Flow

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CACHE STRUCTURE                           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  localStorage['stablepay_transactions'] = {                      │
│    transactions: [...],      // Serialized tx array              │
│    lastSyncedBlock: "7234567", // For incremental fetch          │
│    merchantAddress: "0x..."    // Cache invalidation key         │
│  }                                                               │
│                                                                  │
│  localStorage['stablepay_merchant_address'] = "0x..."            │
│                                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 📊 Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Full blockchain scan on every fetch | Yes | No (incremental) |
| Data persists across refresh | No (5min expiry) | Yes (indefinite) |
| Wallet-specific filtering | No | Yes |
| Deduplication | No | Yes |
| SSR safe | No | Yes |


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/78c44920-c5b8-4a8e-9f01-4bc28658f13b

  * Added "Fetch Fresh Data" option for transaction refresh.
  * Transaction details now include Etherscan viewing capability.
  * Last synced block information displayed in transaction page.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved transaction deduplication and data persistence.

---

## Summary

This PR implements a caching layer for the Overview tab statistics, ensuring instant data display on page load while seamlessly updating when fresh blockchain data is fetched.

---

## Changes

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `dashboard/lib/stats-cache.ts` | LocalStorage caching utilities |
| `dashboard/hooks/use-stats.ts` | Stats calculation & cache management hook |
| `dashboard/app/page.tsx` | Overview page with cached stats integration |
| `dashboard/components/dashboard/chart/index.tsx` | Chart with real transaction data support |

---

## Architecture

### Caching Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                             │
│    ┌──────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐      │
│    │              │         │              │         │              │      │
│    │  Page Load   │────────▶│ Check Cache  │────────▶│   Display    │      │
│    │              │         │ (localStorage)│         │              │      │
│    └──────────────┘         └──────┬───────┘         └──────────────┘      │
│                                    │                                        │
│                         ┌──────────┴──────────┐                            │
│                         │                     │                            │
│                         ▼                     ▼                            │
│                  ┌─────────────┐       ┌─────────────┐                     │
│                  │  Has Cache  │       │  No Cache   │                     │
│                  │             │       │             │                     │
│                  │ Show Values │       │  Show T/A   │                     │
│                  │  Instantly  │       │  + Banner   │                     │
│                  └─────────────┘       └─────────────┘                     │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                             │
│                         User Triggers Fetch                                 │
│                      (Transactions Tab Button)                              │
│                                │                                            │
│                                ▼                                            │
│                  ┌─────────────────────────────┐                           │
│                  │                             │                           │
│                  │   Show Loading Indicator    │                           │
│                  │   (Keep Cached Values)      │                           │
│                  │                             │                           │
│                  └──────────────┬──────────────┘                           │
│                                 │                                           │
│                                 ▼                                           │
│                  ┌─────────────────────────────┐                           │
│                  │                             │                           │
│                  │   Calculate New Stats       │                           │
│                  │   Update Cache + Display    │                           │
│                  │                             │                           │
│                  └─────────────────────────────┘                           │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### State Machine

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                             │
│                              State Transitions                              │
│                                                                             │
│    ┌───────────┐      ┌───────────┐      ┌───────────┐      ┌───────────┐  │
│    │           │      │           │      │           │      │           │  │
│    │ HYDRATING │─────▶│ NO_CACHE  │─────▶│  CACHED   │─────▶│REFRESHING │  │
│    │           │      │           │      │           │      │           │  │
│    │    ...    │      │    T/A    │      │  Values   │      │ Values +  │  │
│    │           │      │           │      │           │      │  Spinner  │  │
│    └───────────┘      └───────────┘      └─────┬─────┘      └─────┬─────┘  │
│                                                │                   │        │
│                                                │                   │        │
│                                                └───────────────────┘        │
│                                                     (on refresh)            │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Stats Displayed

| Metric | Source | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Transactions Processed | `transactions.length` | Total transaction count |
| Revenue Generated | `Σ amountBC` | Sum of all transaction amounts (ETH) |
| Success Rate | `100%` | All blockchain transactions succeed |
| Failed Transactions | `0` | No failures in confirmed transactions |
| Pending Transactions | `0` | All transactions are confirmed |

---

## Implementation Details

### Cache Structure

```typescript
interface PlatformStats {
  transactionsProcessed: number;
  revenueGenerated: number;
  successRate: number;
  failedTransactions: number;
  pendingTransactions: number;
  lastUpdated: number;  // Unix timestamp
}
```

### Hook API

```typescript
const { stats, hasCachedData, isHydrated, isRefreshing } = useStats(transactions, loading);
```

---

## Testing Checklist

- [x] Build succeeds without errors
- [x] Stats display `...` during SSR hydration
- [x] Stats display `T/A` when no cache exists
- [x] Cached values appear instantly on page load
- [x] Loading indicator shows during data refresh
- [x] Cached values persist through page refresh
- [x] Chart updates with real transaction data
- [x] No UI flicker during state transitions

---

## Acceptance Criteria

| Requirement | Status |
|-------------|:------:|
| Overview tab shows stats immediately on load | ✅ |
| Page refresh does not clear displayed values | ✅ |
| Stats and graph update after fetch completes | ✅ |
| No UI flicker or empty state during loading | ✅ |
| Changes scoped to Overview tab only | ✅ |

---

## Screenshots

> Stats display with cached data and loading states

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  TRANSACTIONS    REVENUE        SUCCESS    FAILED     PENDING     │
│  PROCESSED       GENERATED      RATE       TRANS.     TRANS.      │
│                                                                    │
│     156          0.0847 ETH     100%         0           0        │
│  ───────────   ─────────────   ───────   ─────────   ─────────    │
│  TOTAL COUNT   TOTAL EARNINGS  SUCCESS   NO FAILURES ALL CONFIRMED│
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Connect Overview stats to local storage cache & implement real chart data
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
This PR addresses the requirement to show cached statistics immediately on the Overview tab while fetching fresh data in the background. It also replaces the static mock data in the main chart with real transaction data.
Fixes #5 

Changes made:

- Instant Loading / Caching: The Overview tab now initializes state from localStorage immediately. Users won't see empty "T/A" states on page refresh anymore.
- Real Chart Data: I removed the hardcoded mock data in index.tsx. I added a generateChartData() utility that groups the actual transactions by week/month/year so the graph reflects real activity.
- Revenue Fix: I noticed the "Revenue Generated" card was hardcoded to return "T/A" in the render logic, so I fixed the calculation to sum up the actual transaction amounts.
- UX Improvements:
    - Added a small "Updating data..." banner/indicator that appears when a background fetch is happening, so the user knows the cached numbers might change.
    - Added the missing LockIcon for the Failed Transactions card.
    
Technical Implementation: 
1. page.tsx: Wired up the useTransactions loading state. The UI now gracefully handles the "stale-while-revalidate" pattern showing cached data while loading is true.

2. index.tsx (stat): Wrapped the data generation in useMemo to prevent unnecessary re-renders when toggling between timeframes. Added handling for empty states (e.g., if a user has 0 transactions, the graph renders a flat line instead of crashing).

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Implement Wallet-Based Transaction Filtering & Persistent Caching - Issue #6
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
### Fixes #6

## 📊 Overview

This PR implements wallet-based transaction filtering and indefinite persistent caching with incremental updates to improve dashboard performance and reduce unnecessary blockchain queries.

🔗 **Reference PR**: #2  
🔗 **Reference Commit**: [c357bb1](https://github.com/DjedAlliance/StablePay-MerchantDashboard/commit/c357bb164e86b4137b4febc762c1442b0afe84ec)

---

## ✅ Features Implemented

### 1. Wallet-Based Transaction Filtering
- ✅ **Automatic Address Capture**: Merchant wallet address is captured automatically on wallet connection
- ✅ **Receiver Filtering**: Only fetches transactions where the merchant is the receiver
- ✅ **Wallet-Specific Cache**: Each wallet address maintains its own transaction cache
- ✅ **Seamless Switching**: Cache automatically switches when changing wallets

### 2. Persistent Caching (Indefinite)
- ✅ **No Expiration**: Transactions stored in `localStorage` persist indefinitely
- ✅ **Instant Load**: Cached data appears immediately on page load
- ✅ **Refresh-Safe**: Data survives page refreshes and browser restarts
- ✅ **Error Resilient**: Failed fetches don't clear existing cache

### 3. Incremental Fetching
- ✅ **Block Tracking**: `lastSyncedBlock` tracks the latest fetched block
- ✅ **Smart Updates**: "Fetch Fresh Data" only retrieves transactions after last synced block
- ✅ **Data Preservation**: New transactions are appended, never replacing existing data
- ✅ **Optimized Queries**: Reduced blockchain API calls and bandwidth usage

---

## 📝 Changes Made

### 1. Updated Hook: `dashboard/hooks/use-transactions.ts`

**Key Updates:**
```typescript
// Integrated wallet connection
import { useWallet } from './use-wallet';
const { walletAddress, isConnected } = useWallet();

// Enhanced cache structure
interface CachedData {
    transactions: TransactionEvent[];
    lastSyncedBlock: string;  // Track last synced block
    merchantAddress: string;   // Wallet-specific caching
}

// Incremental fetch support
const fetchTransactions = async (incremental = false) => {
    const fromBlock = incremental && lastSyncedBlock 
        ? lastSyncedBlock + 1n 
        : undefined;
    
    const events = await transactionService.fetchStableCoinPurchases(
        walletAddress,  // Filter by merchant address
        fromBlock       // Incremental from last block
    );
    
    // Append new transactions to existing cache
    if (incremental && transactions.length > 0) {
        updatedTransactions = [...transactions, ...events];
    }
};
```

**Features:**
- Wallet address integration for filtering
- Wallet-specific cache validation
- Incremental fetch with block tracking
- Append-only transaction updates
- Indefinite caching (removed expiry)

### 2. Enhanced Service: `dashboard/lib/transaction-service.ts`

**Method Signature:**
```typescript
async fetchStableCoinPurchases(
    merchantAddress?: string,  // Filter by merchant wallet
    fromBlock?: bigint        // Start from specific block
): Promise<TransactionEvent[]>
```

**Implementation:**
```typescript
const purchaseEvents = await this.publicClient.getLogs({
    address: getCurrentContractAddress(),
    event: parseAbiItem('event BoughtStableCoins(...)'),
    args: merchantAddress ? {
        receiver: merchantAddress  // Filter by receiver
    } : undefined,
    fromBlock: from,   // Support incremental fetching
    toBlock: to
});
```

**Features:**
- Merchant address filtering at blockchain level
- Incremental fetching from specific block
- Transaction sorting by block number
- Enhanced logging for debugging

### 3. New Documentation: `dashboard/WALLET_FILTERING_CACHING.md`

Comprehensive documentation including:
- Feature overview and benefits
- Implementation details
- Data flow diagrams
- Usage examples
- Testing checklist
- Known limitations
- Future enhancements

---

## 🔄 Data Flow

```
1. Wallet Connects
   └─> Capture merchant address
   └─> Load cached transactions for this wallet (if exists)
   └─> Restore lastSyncedBlock

2. Initial Fetch
   └─> fetchTransactions(incremental = false)
   └─> Fetch ALL transactions where merchant is receiver
   └─> Store in localStorage with lastSyncedBlock

3. Incremental Fetch ("Fetch Fresh Data")
   └─> fetchTransactions(incremental = true)
   └─> Fetch ONLY transactions after lastSyncedBlock
   └─> Append new transactions to existing cache
   └─> Update lastSyncedBlock to latest

4. Page Refresh
   └─> Cache loads instantly from localStorage
   └─> lastSyncedBlock is restored
   └─> Ready for next incremental update
```

---

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Only merchant-relevant transactions are displayed
- [x] Merchant address is captured on wallet connect
- [x] Transactions are filtered by receiver address
- [x] Cached transactions persist across refreshes
- [x] Cache is indefinite (no expiration)
- [x] `lastSyncedBlock` is tracked and stored
- [x] Incremental fetch works based on `lastSyncedBlock`
- [x] No previously fetched data is lost on refresh or refetch
- [x] New transactions are appended, not replaced
- [x] Cache is wallet-specific

---

## 📦 Files Changed

1. **`dashboard/hooks/use-transactions.ts`**
   - Integrated wallet address for filtering
   - Implemented incremental fetching
   - Added wallet-specific caching
   - Removed cache expiration

2. **`dashboard/lib/transaction-service.ts`**
   - Added `merchantAddress` parameter
   - Added `fromBlock` parameter for incremental fetch
   - Enhanced filtering at blockchain level
   - Added transaction sorting

3. **`dashboard/WALLET_FILTERING_CACHING.md`** (New)
   - Comprehensive implementation documentation
   - Usage examples and testing checklist

---

## 🚀 Benefits

### Performance
- **Instant Load**: Cached data appears immediately on page load
- **Reduced API Calls**: Only fetches new transactions, not entire history
- **Bandwidth Savings**: Incremental fetches use minimal data
- **Optimized Queries**: Blockchain queries limited to new blocks only

### User Experience
- **No Data Loss**: Previously fetched transactions never disappear
- **Smooth Updates**: New data appends without UI flicker
- **Wallet-Specific**: Each wallet maintains its own transaction history
- **Refresh-Safe**: Page refreshes don't clear data

### Reliability
- **Persistent Storage**: Data survives page refreshes and browser restarts
- **Error Recovery**: Failed fetches don't clear existing cache
- **Wallet Switching**: Automatically loads correct cache for connected wallet

---

## 🧪 Testing Notes

### Test Environment Requirements
If your connected wallet doesn't have transactions:
1. Visit the merchant demo website and make minimal transactions
2. Or go to https://mordor.djed.one and buy stablecoins
3. This ensures you can see transactions related to your address

### Testing Checklist

#### Wallet Connection
- [ ] Connect wallet and verify address is captured
- [ ] Check if existing cache loads for connected wallet
- [ ] Switch wallets and verify cache switches

#### Initial Fetch
- [ ] Click "Fetch Transactions" when no cache exists
- [ ] Verify transactions are filtered by merchant address
- [ ] Confirm `lastSyncedBlock` is stored
- [ ] Check localStorage contains cached data

#### Incremental Fetch
- [ ] After initial fetch, click "Fetch Fresh Data"
- [ ] Verify only new transactions are fetched
- [ ] Confirm new transactions are appended
- [ ] Check `lastSyncedBlock` updates

#### Persistence
- [ ] Refresh page (F5)
- [ ] Verify transactions load instantly
- [ ] Confirm count matches before refresh
- [ ] Check `lastSyncedBlock` is restored

---

## 📚 Cache Structure

### LocalStorage Key: `stablepay_transactions`
```json
{
  "transactions": [
    {
      "buyer": "0x1234...",
      "receiver": "0x5678...",
      "amountSC": "1000",
      "amountBC": "0.5",
      "blockNumber": "7845123",
      "transactionHash": "0xabcd..."
    }
  ],
  "lastSyncedBlock": "7845123",
  "merchantAddress": "0x5678..."
}
```

---

## 🔍 Known Limitations

1. **Cache Size**: Large transaction histories may exceed localStorage limits (~5-10MB)
2. **No Deduplication**: Duplicate transactions (same hash) are not automatically filtered
3. **No Expiration**: Cache never expires automatically
4. **Single Device**: Cache is browser-specific, not synced across devices

---

## 🔮 Future Enhancements

- [ ] Add transaction deduplication by hash
- [ ] Implement cache size monitoring and cleanup
- [ ] Add optional cache expiration for data freshness
- [ ] Support for multiple wallet addresses simultaneously
- [ ] Export/import cache functionality
- [ ] Server-side caching for cross-device sync

---

**Implementation Date**: December 13, 2025  
**Status**: ✅ Ready for Review and Testing

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Feat: Implement Wallet Filtering, Persistent Caching, and Real-Time Overview Analytics
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
## Summary
This PR implements wallet-based transaction filtering, persistent caching, and a fully dynamic Overview dashboard. Also configured GitHub Actions for static deployment to GitHub Pages.

**Note:** This issue was resolved during the Unstoppable Hackathon timeperiod.

## Key Changes

### 1. Core Logic (Caching & Filtering)
- **Service:** Updated `transaction-service.ts` to support incremental fetching (`fromBlock`).
- **Hook:** Updated `useTransactions` to cache data in `localStorage`. Data persists across refreshes.
- **Config:** Fixed Wagmi config to correctly support MetaMask injection.

### 2. UI Updates (Overview & Sidebar)
- **Overview Tab:**
    - Now displays **real-time statistics** (Revenue, Success Rate, Tx Count) calculated from cached data.
    - Stats load **instantly** on page refresh (no flicker).
    - Added "T/A" states for when data is not yet fetched.
- **Dynamic Graph:** Updated `chart.tsx` to visualize the actual transaction history instead of mock data.
- **Sidebar Fix:** Resolved a hydration error by adding a client-side mount check for the wallet button.

## Verification
- verified that stats on the Overview page match the fetched transaction data.
- Confirmed that graphs update dynamically when new transactions are fetched.
- Verified that all data persists after a browser refresh.

  * Wallet UI now defers until client mount for more reliable connection display

* **Improvements**
  * Incremental transaction sync with improved persistence and a clear-cache capability
  * Better no-data messaging and header descriptions; removed unnecessary font preloading

### Fixes #4

## 🚀 Overview
This PR implements GitHub Pages deployment for the StablePay Merchant Dashboard. The website has been successfully deployed and tested.

🔗 **Live Demo**: https://manishyad375375.github.io/StablePay-MerchantDashboard/

---

## 📝 Changes Made

### 1. Next.js Configuration (`dashboard/next.config.mjs`)
Configured Next.js for static export and GitHub Pages compatibility:

```javascript
output: "export",              // Enable static HTML export
basePath: "/StablePay-MerchantDashboard",  // Set proper routing for GitHub Pages
images: { unoptimized: true }  // Required for static export
```

**Changes:**
- ✅ Added `output: "export"` to generate static HTML files
- ✅ Set `basePath: "/StablePay-MerchantDashboard"` for correct GitHub Pages routing
- ✅ Kept existing `typescript.ignoreBuildErrors` and `images.unoptimized` settings

### 2. GitHub Actions Workflow (`.github/workflows/nextjs.yml`)
Updated deployment workflow to support the `dashboard/` subdirectory structure:

**Key Updates:**
- ✅ Modified package manager detection to check `dashboard/package.json`
- ✅ Added `cache-dependency-path: dashboard/package-lock.json` for proper caching
- ✅ Changed cache path to `dashboard/.next/cache`
- ✅ Added `working-directory: ./dashboard` for install and build steps
- ✅ Updated artifact upload path to `./dashboard/out`

---

## ✅ Testing & Verification

### Deployment Testing
- [x] Static export builds successfully
- [x] All routes load correctly with basePath
- [x] Assets (images, CSS, JS) load properly
- [x] Wallet connection functionality works
- [x] Dashboard features are fully accessible
- [x] GitHub Actions workflow completes without errors
- [x] Tested on fork before creating PR

### Live Site Verification
The deployment has been thoroughly tested at the fork's GitHub Pages URL. All features work as expected:
- Dashboard loads correctly
- Routes navigate properly
- Wallet integration functions
- Transaction display works
- Overview charts render

---

## 🔧 Technical Details

### Build Process
```bash
cd dashboard
npm ci
npx next build
# Static files generated in: dashboard/out/
```

### Deployment Workflow
1. Push to `main` branch triggers GitHub Actions
2. Workflow checks out code and sets up Node.js 20
3. Installs dependencies with caching from `dashboard/`
4. Builds Next.js app in static export mode
5. Uploads artifact from `dashboard/out/`
6. Deploys to GitHub Pages
7. Site becomes live at `https://djedalliance.github.io/StablePay-MerchantDashboard/`

---

## 📋 Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Next.js site deploys successfully on GitHub Pages
- [x] Routes and assets load correctly
- [x] Deployment works via GitHub Actions
- [x] PR contains only deployment-related changes
- [x] No modifications to core application functionality
- [x] Tested and verified in fork

---

## 📦 Files Changed

1. **`dashboard/next.config.mjs`**
   - Added static export configuration
   - Set basePath for GitHub Pages routing

2. **`.github/workflows/nextjs.yml`**
   - Updated to support dashboard subdirectory
   - Fixed paths for caching and artifact upload
   - Added working directory for build commands

---

## 🎯 Key Features

- **Automatic Deployment**: Pushes to main trigger deployment
- **Fast Builds**: Caching improves build times
- **Static Export**: No server required, works on GitHub Pages
- **Clean URLs**: basePath ensures proper routing
- **Production Ready**: Tested and verified

---

## 📚 Additional Notes

- The `basePath` configuration ensures all internal links work correctly under the repository path
- Image optimization is already disabled (required for static export)
- The workflow uses npm for consistency with the project setup
- Cache configuration reduces subsequent build times significantly
- No changes to application logic or functionality

---

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Implement Overview Tab Stats Caching with Loading Behavior
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
## 📊 Overview

This PR implements localStorage caching for the Overview tab statistics, ensuring that stats display immediately on page load and update gracefully when fresh data is fetched.

## ✨ Features Implemented

- ✅ **Instant display** of cached stats on page load
- ✅ **T/A default values** shown until first data fetch completes
- ✅ **Refresh-safe behavior** - cached data persists across page reloads
- ✅ **Loading indicators** that don't hide cached values
- ✅ **Graceful error handling** - failed fetches don't clear cached data
- ✅ **Auto-update** of stats and graph when fresh data arrives
- ✅ **No UI flicker** - smooth transitions between states

## 📝 Changes Made

### New Files Created

1. **`dashboard/lib/stats-cache.ts`**
   - Manages localStorage caching for overview statistics
   - Provides functions: `getCachedStats()`, `setCachedStats()`, `clearStatsCache()`, `hasCachedStats()`, `getFormattedUpdateTime()`

2. **`dashboard/lib/calculate-stats.ts`**
   - Calculates statistics from transaction data
   - Functions: `calculateStatsFromTransactions()`, `formatRevenue()`, `formatSuccessRate()`

3. **`dashboard/OVERVIEW_STATS_IMPLEMENTATION.md`**
   - Comprehensive documentation with usage examples and testing checklist

### Modified Files

1. **`dashboard/app/page.tsx`**
   - Added state management for stats and loading indicators
   - Integrated cache loading on component mount
   - Implemented automatic stats update when transactions are fetched

2. **`dashboard/components/dashboard/chart/index.tsx`**
   - Updated to accept dynamic transaction data via props
   - Processes real transaction data into chart format
   - Falls back to mock data when no transactions exist

## 📊 Stats Displayed

| Stat | Description | Source |
|------|-------------|--------|
| **Transactions Processed** | Total number of transactions | Count of transaction array |
| **Revenue Generated** | Total revenue in BC | Sum of `amountBC` from transactions |
| **Success Rate** | Percentage of successful transactions | 100% for blockchain transactions |
| **Failed Transactions** | Number of failed transactions | Currently 0 (blockchain data) |
| **Pending Transactions** | Number of pending transactions | Currently 0 (blockchain data) |

## 🔄 Data Flow

```
1. Component Mount → 2. Load cached stats from localStorage
3. Display cached values (or T/A if no cache)
4. When transactions are fetched → 5. Calculate fresh stats
6. Show loading indicator (keeping cached values visible)
7. Update UI with fresh data → 8. Save to localStorage cache
```

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Overview tab shows stats immediately on load
- [x] Page refresh does not clear displayed values
- [x] Stats and graph update after fetch completes
- [x] No UI flicker or empty state during loading
- [x] Cached data is for UX only (not a source of truth)
- [x] Changes scoped to Overview tab

## 📦 Cache Structure

```typescript
interface OverviewStats {
  transactionsProcessed: number;
  revenueGenerated: number;
  successRate: number;
  failedTransactions: number;
  pendingTransactions: number;
  lastUpdated: number; // Unix timestamp
}
```

Stored in localStorage: `stablepay_overview_stats`

## 🔧 Integration

- Uses existing `useTransactions` hook
- Integrates with existing transaction service
- Works with existing refresh button in Transactions tab
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

## 📚 Documentation

Complete implementation documentation available in `dashboard/OVERVIEW_STATS_IMPLEMENTATION.md`

## 🐛 Known Limitations

- Failed/pending transactions currently show as 0 (blockchain data limitation)
- Cache doesn't expire automatically (indefinite storage)

## 🔍 Code Quality

- TypeScript strict mode compliance
- Proper error handling
- SSR-safe (checks for `window` object)
- Memoized calculations for performance
- Clean separation of concerns

---

**Implementation Date**: December 13, 2025  
**Status**: ✅ Ready for Review

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Feat: Wallet Filtering & Persistent Caching
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
## Summary
This PR implements wallet-based transaction filtering and persistent caching to optimize the merchant dashboard. It also resolves critical UI hydration errors and fixes the wallet connection configuration.

**Note:** This issue was resolved during the Unstoppable Hackathon timeperiod.

## Key Changes implemented

### 1. Feature: Wallet-Based Filtering & Caching
* **Service Update (`transaction-service.ts`):** Updated the service to accept `address` and `fromBlock` parameters, enabling filtering at the API level.
* **Smart Hook (`use-transactions.ts`):**
    * Implemented `localStorage` caching logic. Data now persists across page refreshes (`F5`).
    * Added **Incremental Fetching**: The app tracks `lastSyncedBlock` and only requests new blocks on subsequent fetches, reducing network load.
    * Added logic to merge new transactions with the existing cached history.

### 2. Bug Fix: UI Hydration Error
* **Sidebar Component (`sidebar/index.tsx`):** Fixed a "Text content did not match" hydration error by introducing a `mounted` state check. This ensures the wallet address in the sidebar only renders on the client-side, preventing server-client mismatch.

### 3. Configuration Fix: Wallet Connection
* **Wagmi Config (`wagmi-config.ts`):** Added the `injected()` connector to correctly detect and trigger MetaMask (and other browser wallets) which was previously failing to pop up.

## Verification / Testing
* **Persistence:** Verified that transaction lists remain visible immediately after a page refresh.
* **Filtering:** Confirmed that clicking "Fetch" sends the correct wallet address and block number (visible in Console logs).
* **Connection:** Verified that the "Connect Wallet" button now successfully triggers the MetaMask popup without errors.

  * Improved wallet connect flow with smarter connector selection.

  * Incremental transaction syncing with per-address caching and last-synced tracking.
  * Network/config updates to streamline build/runtime behavior.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Guarded wallet UI to avoid client-mount rendering issues.
  * Safer chart/empty-state handling when no data is available.

StablePay-MerchantDashboard.title: Add Transaction Filtering + Persistent Caching Logic
StablePay-MerchantDashboard.body:
### Description
Introduce **wallet-based transaction filtering** and **persistent caching** to improve performance and reduce unnecessary blockchain queries.

This builds on the previous work introduced in:
🔗 https://github.com/DjedAlliance/StablePay-MerchantDashboard/pull/2


additional reference: https://github.com/DjedAlliance/StablePay-MerchantDashboard/commit/c357bb164e86b4137b4febc762c1442b0afe84ec


if your connected wallet does not have any transactions related to djed stablecoins or stablepay sdk ,then go the merhcantdemo website and make some minimal transaction ,or go to the https://mordor.djed.one and buy stablecoins ,so as to see the trasnactions related to your address on the dashboard.

---

### Key Changes

#### Transaction Filtering
- Filter transactions by **merchant wallet address**
- Merchant address should be captured **once on wallet connect**
- Only fetch transactions where the **merchant is the receiver** 
 (*_*the code already has a placeholder and the functionality is done ,you only need to pass the address here )**_
- Pass merchant address into `TransactionService` for filtering


#### Persistent Caching
- Store fetched transactions in `localStorage` (persist across refreshes)
- Cache should be **indefinite** (until explicitly refreshed)
- Track `lastSyncedBlock` to support incremental updates

#### Incremental Fetching
- On **"Fetch Fresh Data"**:
  - Fetch only transactions **after `lastSyncedBlock`**
  - Append new transactions to existing cache
  - Do not refetch or discard previously cached data

---

### Flow
1. Wallet connects → merchant address is captured
2. Transactions are fetched where merchant is the receiver
3. Results are cached in `localStorage`
4. `lastSyncedBlock` is stored
5. On refresh, cached data is shown instantly
6. Fresh fetch only pulls new transactions and appends them

---

### Benefits
- Reduces unnecessary blockchain queries
- Improves dashboard performance and reliability
- Enables scalable future enhancements (deduplication, better error handling)

---

### Acceptance Criteria
- Only merchant-relevant transactions are displayed
- Cached transactions persist across refreshes
- Incremental fetch works based on `lastSyncedBlock`
- No previously fetched data is lost on refresh or refetch
